channel.title: R on Le Cahier
channel.description: Recent content in R on Le Cahier

item.title: Classification using SVM: Beginner's Tale in R
item.description: This is literally the first time I’ve ever tried using SVM. I’m not so big on all this “Machine Learning” thing; most of the time I use R merely for data analysis, statistics, and visualizations. But after careful reading it seems that SVM could be really useful for quick data classification, especially for predicting if the result is going to be binary (Yes or No, A or B, 1 or 0, etc.

item.title: How to plot a linear regression in R
item.description: Intro A more compatible title perhaps would be: How to plot an Excel-like linear regression in R. This is a half-note, half-tutorial that I wrote partly because lately I keep forgetting it, something that I’m actually a bit ashamed, although it seems apparently to be quite normal among data scientists to forget some basic things, like reading CSV files. I suppose a background is needed. I first started to use R because I’m interested in its visualization capability.

item.title: Leaflet Test
item.description: This is my first blog post featuring a leaflet map. I’ve followed the step-by-step guide provided in this blog post and apparently it works. To say that I’m happy would be an understatement. Now I’m able to write blog post with maps embedded in it. I’m going to look up how to “beautify” this website though; perhaps I’m going to find some custom themes.
